text: Moved here from Louisiana recently the tukwilla area to be exact. I have 4 kids between 2-12. Looking for recommendations of things to do around the area for free or low cost. Back home we had splash pads everywhere’s. They like beaches hiking and parks.

attachments.ocrText: May be an image of beach and text that says '10:20 Zachary Cox is at Beautiful Lake Washington. May 30 2 Write a comment... GIF'

topComments.text: Theres a splash parks near Angle Lake and another small one for smaller kids at the Tukwila Communitu Center. Every first Thursday of the month, there is Free Museum day, so take advantage of that. The museum of flight is great one but its endless if youre willing to drive to Seattle.

The aquarium,zoo, and other place like the museum are free or only 5 bucks if you have wic or ebt . There’s a bunch of splash water parks in the Seattle area we always at parks or looking for new things to do also, that's amazing! 🌟

text: In 6 days, the World Cup starts, and it’s happening right here in Seattle. Honestly, I’m expecting chaos. text:
Today we did another small but meaningful thing for our community 💪
A great group of people came together for a community clean-up, and once again it proved that small actions really do make a difference.

Some may see it as “just picking up trash,” but keeping our neighborhoods clean starts with people who care enough to show up. It was especially great seeing both adults and kids helping side by side — setting an example that every little effort matters.

In just a couple of hours, we collected several large bags of trash and made the area noticeably cleaner. A huge thank you to everyone who joined, helped, and gave their time 🙌

Small actions = big impact.

Looking forward to the next clean-up! Who’s in? ♻️🇺🇸

text:
Hi everyone! 

My daughter and I recently moved to Seattle from Memphis, as I'll be starting my Neonatology fellowship in July. We're excited for this new chapter, but we're definitely still getting settled and figuring everything out.

I'm looking for elementary school recommendations for my daughter, who will be starting 2nd grade this fall. She's a sweet, creative, outgoing girl who loves making friends and trying new things. She also has ADHD, so I'd love to find a school with supportive teachers and a welcoming environment where she can really thrive.

Since we're new here, I'd also appreciate any recommendations for clubs, sports, activities, or other opportunities where she can meet kids her age and start making some friends. We'd love to get involved in the community and help her feel at home before the school year starts.

Thank you so much for any suggestions I really appreciate it! ❤️

topComments.text: Shoreline, Northshore and Lake Washington  all have good school district if you are looking to move.
hubby teaches in Kirkland ! lovely school district....

text: Going on a cruise in a few weeks and leaving out of Seattle. Cruise leaves on a Friday and plan on flying in late Wednesday. Is it possible to do the zoo, aquarium and space needle all in one day? 2 adults.

topComments.text:
If you want to see ALL of the zoo, then no. The zoo + Space Needle OR aquarium + Space Needle are both much better bets. 
Also : don’t overlook the Chihuly Glass Museum/Garden right next to the Space Needle. Highly recommend.
I would do 2 before you leave and try to do 1 when u get back

text: Why does Seattle wants tip for everything?! I’m so upset if I’m just doing take out bro I’m already paying for my food

topComments.text:
When we were in Vegas they even requested tips at retail stores.  They would turn the card machine around and say, “There’s a question I need you to answer.”

Why would I tip when I went in shopped and selected some items?  All they did was run the cash register.

I’ve worked in the industry here for 20y plus 🙋🏻‍♂️🙋🏻‍♂️🙋🏻‍♂️ … I can tell you a bunch of valid reason why and why not tipping is part of this society, not only in the restaurant business but Uber, barber shops, nail salon etc … but it’ll be irrelevant and waste of time to try to explain to you! Just keep in mind that everything out there is getting expensive specially in the restaurant industry! Can’t afford it?!?! don’t go out as simple as that so you don’t feel obligated to tip to appreciate the service provide and that includes Take-Out

text: I was parked under the arch way in China town and a baby bird fell out of his nest. He ran right under my car and hid. I called animal control and paws wildlife center but they are both closed. I put him in the bushes nearby. Is anyone able to help this baby?

attachments.ocrText: May be an image of temple
May be an image of chipmunk and rabbit
May be an image of turtle, lovebird and grass

topComments.text:
Pidgey is safe! I have the supplies needed to care for him over the weekend until a wild life rescue can take him (I’m a vet tech). I ended up taking him with me after assessing the situation; babies that fall from nests in dense urban areas tend not to do so great, and it didn’t seem like he was in an area where mom could easily find him if he’d be protected from people and dogs. 
He looks to be in good health; his crop still has some food in it so mom had fed him recently before he fell from the nest.

attachments.ocrText: No photo description available.

topComments.text: Leave him close to where you found him. His momma will find him better that way. But if you leave him on the ground he likely will get eaten by a cat. Might want to watch him for a while

text: Hello Seattle friends! My partner and I are looking for a car rental company that allows one way drop-offs from SeaTac to Vancouver, Canada. Unfortunately, everyone we called seemed to not be able to do this. We need help finding direction to look for an option to get back home! We are arriving in Seattle around 12 midnight tonight and hoping to drive down to Canada when we land. Any leads will help, thank you Seattle!

text:
Hi all! My family and I are planning a move from PA to Seattle. My son will be in a PhD program and hopefully working at a museum. We are looking for a place that is outside the city, but has easy access to the city. I own a small baked goods/candy business (I'll be bringing some Pittsburgh Polish goodies!)
Any suggestions on neighborhoods? (We will be renting at first). 

I should add, I know Washington state in general is more expensive. We are prepared for that.

Hi all! My family and I are planning a move from PA to Seattle. My son will be in a PhD program and hopefully working at a museum. We are looking for a place that is outside the city, but has easy access to the city. I own a small baked goods/candy business (I'll be bringing some Pittsburgh Polish goodies!)

topComments.text: Seattle or North or Eastside stay away from any city south of Seattle

Shoreline, Kenmore, Lake Forest Park, and Edmonds are good places to plan on looking at!! Lightrail makes access to the city very easy, but if you prefer to drive then Shoreline or Lake Forest Park would be great - they are relatively close to the freeway. Edmonds can be close or quite a drive to the freeway - but it's a beautiful area to live in!

text: Can anyone from Seattle give me reccomendations for good seafood, burger and buffet restaurants??

topComments.text: Spuds on Alki, great fish and chips with the best view in the world!

Ivar’s Salmon House or 
Ray’s Boathouse for seafood. Spuds on Alki or Sunfish on Alki are good and more casual. 
Salty’s does a brunch buffet as does Snoqualmie Casino. 
We also like the Asian buffet at the Feast in Renton. 
Dick’s is an iconic Seattle burger place. Nothing fancy, but we like the burgers and fries.

text: Going to Spokane and seattle in a few weeks. We will spend about 2 days each. What are some "MUST see" scenic places/parks and some restaurant recommendations that will be worthwhile in our short time? TIA

topComments.text:
In Seattle 
I'd recommend 
The shops on waterfront (YeOldCururiosityShop)
The Pike Place is also filled with all kinds of shops.
The Burke Museum is nice.(UniversityDistrict)
The Aquarium on the waterfront.
But a Lil further north is Woodland Park Zoo.
The Smith Tower in downtown Seattle is awesome!

text:
We’re staying near Wedgwood… and this guy just casually walks out into the road 😳

I had no idea there were areas like this tucked into neighborhoods — it literally drops off into this wooded ravine like a hidden nature corridor

apparently this is a normal thing out here… but it definitely caught me off guard 😅

have you ever seen a coyote just out in the middle of a neighborhood like this?

topComments.text: Lol, yeah I saw a bear on my way to Duval one time
He was chasing the Roadrunner until he read that sign

text: They shut this hill down when it snows 🤒

attachments.ocrText: May be an image of trolley, snowplow, road and text

topComments.text: Queen Anne hill, Seattle
A lot more than that one! I lived on Capital Hill!

text: If you were making a “must see” list for Seattle what would make the Top 5 places to visit?

topComments.text: Chihuly Garden and Glass, Bill Speidel’s Underground Tour, Pike Place Market, MoPop, Smith Tower, Seattle waterfront.
Space needle and gum ball wall and that whole area!

text: Flying in tomorrow. Any recommendations for bars around Seattle? Or a fun place for a Monday evening. I'm only in town for two nights.

topComments.text: I love eating at Vons 1000 Spirits on 1st Ave when in town. Lots of good bars around Belltown.
2 nights on a Monday, yikes

text: Moving to Capitol Hill next month — any tips on neighborhoods to check out?

I live in Maryland, some friends have highly recommended me to move to Seattle They say that we can have a better job and generate more money compared here, I feel afraid since it is not easy. We are a family of 3, 2 adults and a child, we must start from 0

What advice could you give me?

topComments.text: Your friends are lying to you.

I'm from Virginia but I've lived in Washington State, and have frequently spent time in Maryland as well as DC (DMV)

Washington State is a great place to move for the job opportunities, scenic views, proximity to major cities, etc but the cost of living is high.

Example: Think about how much people pay to live in Washington DC but add in higher gas prices

Definitely take time to visit and explore all over Seattle as well as it's neighboring areas before you fully invest, it's a huge cultural shift from the east coast as is but I definitely loved my personal experience and plan to move back

text: Hi! Im traveling to Seattle in August for an Alaska cruise. I was wondering if anyone in this group offers private transportation to the cruise port? Thank you!

topComments.text: Uber. If you’re staying at a downtown hotel they might have a shuttle or a concierge that can arrange for transportation. If you’re staying at a hotel outside of the city, The hotels that are near the airport that have shuttle services do not offer the shuttle to downtown piers.
You can easily take an Uber or Lyft.

text: Coffee time ☕️

attachments.ocrText: May be an image of drink and text

topComments.text: Funny thing.  That's not the actual first location (which no longer exists).
Si el primero de la cadena,me dijeron!!!

text:
3 days in Seattle!
2 adults, no kids. We’re spontaneous and want to pack in as much as we can. Any recommendations for sites, food, hikes. Is Mt Rainier hike-able right now? If we just stay within Seattle, do we need a car? TIA!

topComments.text: Mopop, space needle, pikes place, HMart, Fremont troll, take the ferry to Bainbridge Island walk around town. Hope you have a great time.

The new downtown Waterfront Park and all that the Waterfront of downtown Seattle has to offer, then head over to the Space Needle to see the exhibits there, if you're young and hip and like the food and microbrew scene head to the Fremont District check on their festivals for the season the dates have fun enjoy oh yeah and after downtown Waterfront head up the hill to Pike Place Market is a must!

text: Wallace Falls Trail

attachments.ocrText: May be an image of waterfall and nature

text: My husband and I will be in Seattle for a single evening. Could you recommend some notable attractions and a suitable establishment for a seafood dinner?

topComments.text: Eating at the top of the space needle was incredible.
If you want a phenomenal view with dinner, you should go to Smith Tower. Check their menu first for seafood. I’m not sure what they have. ￼

text:
Hi there 😊
My 15-year-old son and I are planning to visit Seattle for about three days in mid-July, and we’re currently looking for a nice place to stay. 
Ideally, it shouldn’t be too expensive and should be a good base for exploring the city. We’d especially love to stay in a lively neighborhood with lots of young people, great restaurants, and a vibrant atmosphere. 
Any recommendations would be really appreciated – thanks so much in advance!

topComments.text: Avoid Travelodge by the space needle. HORRIBLE horrible people, horrible management, we had a terrible time and it ruined our entire trip. We didn’t even stay an entire day..
Maybe stay a little out of Seattle and take the Light rail to downtown

topComments.text: Wow.  Beautiful. 🤩 we will be in Seattle the end of June.

I have collected the framed posters starting from 1989 almost consecutive to 2006. I'm only missing a couple. They are proudly displayed on the walls of my spare room.  Every one of them has been purchased at either the Goodwill or a Thrift Shop.

sharedPost.text: Either a funnel cloud or tornado over Elliott Bay from Golden Gardens just now.  Came and went several times. #komoweather

attachments.ocrText: May be an image of tornado

text: Back Then I Loved getting dressed up to go downtown shopping, my has it changed 🥰🤗🥰

attachments.ocrText: May be an image of trolley, cablecar, street and text

topComments.text:
From the Pike Place Market to Broadway easy, 
Not today, a convoluted mess......
I remember the bon Marche, My mother lived in Seattle, We used to go shopping there all the time, She used to take me to pikes marketplace all the time I remember the good old days

attachments.label: Animated yellow face emoji, rubbing its chin in a pondering like manner.

text: Hi! I seen a guy wearing a Black and Red Seahawks jersey a few months ago. He said it was a Strangers Thing collaboration jersey. All the ones I see online are from super risky scammer websites. Has anyone here purchased one that they're happy with and wouldnt mind sharing the website?

topComments.text: Ask on  Seattle Seahawk Fans

text: Hi all. Thanks for the add. We’re Floridians who love Seattle and are going there for the 3rd time next month. Can you guys tell me which islands are the best for folks with mobility issues? We went to Bainbridge on the last trip which we loved but was a bit of a struggle for me with bad knees. We want to try and visit a few of them on this trip as long as there are buses or trolleys or uber lol. TIA!

topComments.text: I always liked Vashon Island during the 60s short ferry ride from Lincoln Park. I don't know what the deal is now, but it was a lot of fun and fairly rustic back then
You could take the new light rail 2 line to Mercer Island. No ferry required.

text: Is it better to rent a house or an apartment? We’re moving from las Vegas, we have 1 kid. Can you please suggest￼ where to live, I’ll be working in Tacoma. Thanks

topComments.text:
Tacoma has a mix of areas, just like any city but there are some really great neighborhoods to rent in depending on what you’re looking for.

Some of the best areas to consider:
 • North Tacoma / Proctor District – Very desirable, safe, walkable, and close to shops, restaurants, and parks
 • Ruston / Point Ruston area – Beautiful waterfront living, newer apartments, great for lifestyle and views
 • University Place – Quiet, suburban feel with good schools and easy access to Tacoma
 • Fircrest – Small, safe community with a neighborhood feel
 • Stadium District – More city vibe, historic charm, close to downtown and great views
 • West End Tacoma – Residential, generally quieter, and more affordable than North Tacoma

If you want something a little more budget-friendly, we can also look at parts of South Tacoma, just depending on the specific street and property.

If you’re open to areas just outside of Tacoma, there are some really great surrounding towns that offer a quieter feel, better schools in some cases, and more value for rent.

Top surrounding areas to consider:
 • Puyallup – More affordable, great for families, strong school district, about 10–15 mins from Tacoma  ￼
 • Lakewood – Budget-friendly, lots of rental options, convenient location  ￼
 • DuPont – Very clean, quiet, newer community, popular with military and commuters  ￼
 • Fife – Close to Tacoma and highways, good for commuters
Spanaway, parkland, tacoma mall area or even north tacoma

Moved to Seattle from Toronto a month ago and it’s my son’s birthday in a few days. It’s his first birthday and I always imagined it to be very special:( the move was unexpected but i still want it to be memorable for us. Can you suggest a place where we can go celebrate? 

We live in the South lake union area, any suggestions around this area are appreciated as we don’t have a car yet.

topComments.text: How old will your son be?  That will change things.  Also what is he into?
Children's museum has a special room for a party. Best place for a first birthday

text: Looking for safe parking for a night for a family with young kids in a van. Thank you🙏

topComments.text: A friend who travels with kids parks in truck stops.
Police station parking lot.

text: If you see these guys just know theyy stealinnnn , crummin around Seattle braking into cars! one of them is Gucci'd out fake chain, the other one is SpongeBobs friend in the knock off retros

attachments.ocrText: May be an image of television
No photo description available.

topComments.text: Lets put these ass wipes away..
Come here Boy

text: I’m staying in Seattle near the baseball stadium and we are on our last day of vacation. My family plays the license plate game and we have every license plate except Hawaii. Does anyone in the immediate area happen to know where I can see a Hawaii license plate? It’s a long shot but hoping some locals are used to seeing one somewhere nearby. We’ve gotten within 1 plate on many vacations and never finished. Thanks in advance.

topComments.text: I am a Floridian and was able to get all 50 plates during snowbird/spring break season without ever leaving the state of Florida last year. Our family played again while we visited home this year and have 49 - we are also missing Hawaii after driving across the country last month. Here is the picture of Hawaii from our 24-25 season last year.

attachments.ocrText: May be an image of text that says 'NAWAS SRW 777 25 01-25 TONYOGROUP TONY GROUPP'

topComments.text: Any navy base would have them

text: Hello everyone! I will be in Seattle end of july and I am looking for the beauties of the town! Give suggestion please

topComments.text:
The end of July is usually gorgeous but you could still encounter some gloom.
I’d make time to see the West Seattle flower house at this time of the year.
Spend some time on Alki beach and at the Junction in West Seattle. Have sushi at Mashiko.
Ballard lochs and the Norge museum

text: Going to Seattle and the surrounding areas in a couple weeks, any recommendations for activities and places to go? I.e small businesses, trails, social settings, and places I generally wouldn’t want to miss, any and all recommendations are greatly appreciated!

topComments.text: Yeah go to totem lake I hear they have a good restaurant over there called Taco Bell. It’s on 124th
The Fremont Troll for a quick selfie

text: The bluest skies you ever seen are in Seattle 💕🎶🎵🎼

topComments.text: We all miss Harry and his beautiful blue eyes. ❤️
My parents used to own a business between 1st & Virginia (then Blanchard) from the 40’s until the 90’s. The market when I was a kid was a pretty scary place. We never went there (late 50’s, 60’s).

text: Gas prices continuing to rise are hitting families in the day-to-day. Commutes cost more, grocery runs feel heavier, and simple plans now need a budget check first. Meanwhile, hardworking people feel overlooked while our future wallets keep taking the hit.

text: Careful riding a train here in Washington Seattle

text: Will be coming to Seattle this summer and am wondering what coffee shops have the best decaf options. Specifically decaf matcha, 🍵✨

topComments.text: Girl matcha can't come decaf... Lol its literally green tea
Since you're going to pay dearly for everything else, enjoy the Decaf in your hotel room. It's probably Starbucks.

text: Had an awesome visit to my hometown last week and a half; only the third time since I moved away in '76 since I've been up there - actually had a few sunny days! May be moving back soon...

topComments.text: Laid back......until it's not
Lovely weather

text: Snoqualmie Falls is so majestic, my favorite shot 😍

attachments.ocrText: May be an image of nature and waterfall

topComments.text: Love these falls
What is the best way to visit the Falls. We will be in Seattle early September. Should we take a tour or is it close enough that we can take an Uber?

text: Summer is coming.  Sunset at Shilshole Marina (last summer).

attachments.ocrText: May be an image of boat, sail and twilight

text: My son would like to go to the Funko Pop store in Everett WA.   We will be in Seattle for our cruise.   We will have all day Saturday to do sightseeing etc.   we plan in taking light rail downtown from airport.  What are the logistics to getting to Everett from there.   I see that a bus goes there?    TIA

topComments.text: Better off renting a car honestly
The "Sounder" aka straight through bus line, North line will run you directly up to Everett. Use your GPS to figure out how to get from the Sounder stop, up to Funko.

text:
Must do’s while visiting?
Pet friendly preferably..

We’ll have 2 well trained dogs with us..

Trails, stores, etc!

We will be down this Thursday and are spending the weekend in this area.

topComments.text: Visit Pike Place market and the water front.
Discovery Park - close to Downtown

text: Traveling Through Washington i enjoyed the Weekend with Seafood And Coffee At Seattle Wharf + Downtown boardwalk With sea ships And that Sea Smell + Boat Tour And Dtown Cruise+

attachments.ocrText: May be an image of monument and text
No photo description available.

topComments.text: The waterfront is pretty nice these days!
In the 50s and 60s, they had counter open to the street, you could yell your order coming down the street, by the time you got there, the order would be ready for you

text:
Our first Spring Fremont Evening Market of the year is here. Swing by after work, grab dinner, and shop with some fabulous local vendors. March 26, 4–8 in Fremont :)

SEE YOU THERE! 

N 35th ST in Fremont, in between Triangle & El Camino

attachments.ocrText: May be an image of text

text: Update he has been found and no longer with us 😢He has been missing since Feb 19,2026

attachments.ocrText: May be an image of one or more people, people smiling and text that says 'This is my cousin. He is missing from the Seattle, Washingtor area. If you can all please share the post, it would mean so much. If any of you have friends or family in that area, please ask them to share. His name is Carlos Ruiz. He is 48 years old. Hei 5'10". He is about 190lbs. He was last seen leaving the Veterans Hospital after he checked himself out. There has been a missing person report made.if anyone does have information please call 509-201-6986 Tap Taptotaglocation to tag location'

topComments.text: Que mal aspecto el paisano
My prayers go out to the family, I am a paralegal handling one of Carlos cases… he was a great guy. I am so sorry for your loss.

text: It’s a lot lower resolution when I upload it to Facebook, I just wanted to post this in case was you and your were curious as to what happened?  I called the police, but I’m not sure that they did anything.

topComments.text: how to get into this place…?
AWESOME!!

text: I will be cruising to Alaska out of Seattle on May 19th and was hoping to have brunch at the Space Needle the morning we ge back, May 26th. When trying to make reservations it’s only open on Fri-Sun through the month of April. Can someone tell me their hours of operation during the month of May? Can you have brunch there on a Tuesday? Thanks in advance

topComments.text: May would be a perfect time to head to the Pike Place Market, go down to the southern end and have brunch at the Athenian. Their Strawberry Shortcake is TO DIE FOR! The view of Elliott Bay is spectacular.
If it doesn’t work out, try Cafe Campagne in Pike Place market. Everything is decadent.

text: We are going to Seattle Sunday, spending a whole week out there! Does anybody have any recommendations of places to eat, places to visit anything fun to do?  Also how is the weather over there? Just need ideas please!

topComments.text: Dine in the Athenian at the Pike Street Market.

Go to the locks! I went there when I visited and enjoyed it.
Also Pike’s Peak.

text:
***THANK YOU TO YOU ALL. Here now enjoying beautiful Seattle.♥️***

Hello everyone. How’s the weather right now over at the Sea-Tac airport? Anxious if our flight will get cancelled again like yesterday.

topComments.text: Its beautiful Sunday sunshine in Seattle now. No more snow.
Clear and partial sunshine

text: Elijah has been missing for over 3 weeks and I’m desperate to find my son.  #Washington please help me find my son #missing

attachments.ocrText: May be an image of one or more people and beard

topComments.text: You need to say what he was wearing last if he was clean-shaven or if this is a current looking picture of him and whether or not he might be depressed drugs alcohol different things like that. That way people keep eyes open in certain areas
I’m so sorry. I hope you find him soon and safe

text: Seattle's first snowfall cap Hill below Summit

text:
Whats your opinion on the old Alaskan way Viaduct ? 
 I do miss it 😭

attachments.ocrText: May be an image of road, skyscraper and street

topComments.text: Born and raised in Seattle and I loved the viaduct!  It may have been ugly but it sure helped me get from West Seattle to Ballard to see my folks in a hurry!  Plus, the view was beautiful!

It was ugly and blocked the view of the seafront. And no it’s not just wealthy condo owners who live within view, there’s a low income housing area there and a shelter whom both now enjoy the view of the ocean every day instead of an ugly viaduct

text: Clear skies, grazing horses, old barns, and majestic Mt. Rainier right in front of me. Pacific Northwest perfection. ❤️ if this view hits different! #nonfollower #followers #trendingnow #fypシ#pnw #mtrainier

text: The Bardahl company in Ballard, Washington, with it's well known lighted sign. Founded in 1939, Bardahl sponsored more than 20 cars in the Indy 500, most recently in 2013, partnered with Ferrari racing as early as 1952, and won many hydroplane championships.  Photo from a recent walk.

text:
Hi! I’ll be traveling to Seattle for the first time in about a week and a half and will be staying for about 4.5 days. I’m staying downtown. I will not be renting a car. 
Current Plan is to check out pike place, Bainbridge island, Chihuly garden, MoPOP and MoHAI. I was told Ballard Locks is a must see. Olympic sculpture park is also on the list. Also doing a day trip to mt rainier. Questions for the group:
- anything on the list that isn’t worth it and can be nixed?
- anything else I should add?
- restaurants/bars/wine bars recommendations? 
- I would like to go to a winery (preferably ste Michelle), but is that feasible considering I won’t be renting a car? 

Thank you in advance for any insight and recs!

topComments.text:
Seattle burnt down to the ground in 1889.  When the rebuilt the city they decided to build it up higher since it had constantly flooded.  There is a tour called “Beneath the Streets” that actually takes you down to parts of the original city that are still there.  It’s amazing!!  
Also hotels downtown will have flyers of all the things you can do in Seattle.  You’re going to love it!
I dont think you can do all of that in 4 days honestly.

text:
I will be flying in solo during december, and am not sure what to experience while i’m there, i will have a full day tuesday & wednesday to explore with some time to spare monday & thursday, i do plan to rent a car. 
Please let me know what you think is most important to visit while im in the area. 
I plan to visit the space needle, park place market, is bainbridge island worth it? frye art museum, sky view observatory, seattle center & tower 
What i’m wondering most is areas to stay, and must do things outside of the city. I don’t mind leaving early to catch all of the views and nature during my trip. Thanks in advance!

topComments.text: I love walking the city and everything mentioned. Save the $ on a car. It’ll cost more than you need. It’s totally a walkable city and you will love it.

Skip the car. Bus and light rail. Ride the ferry to Bainbridge, walk around town, ferry back. Good enough! Seattle Art Museum, take the Underground Tour, helps understand how the city was born. When at Pike Place Market have lunch in the Athenian restaurant- great food, scenes filmed there in “Sleepless In Seattle”!  Eat  Dungeness crab, sweet deliciousness……. Buy Market Spice tea to take home. The store is not far from the Athenian.  Eating in the Space Needle is waaaay overpriced. DO visit the Chihuly Glass exhibit near the foot of the Needle - spectacular! Walk around the Center, home of the 1962 Worlds Fair and many original features/buildings there.

text:
Please repost in all seattle neighborhoods.
My daugther is missing... Her name is Violet Benjamin, dob 5-10-1997, 5'10 1/2" tall, slim build...  brown hair, brown eyes. 

She is a very vulnerable adult and is missing her meds.  She was last seen in Ballard yesterday day by Swedish Hospital on Tallman. She suffers from severe depression, anxiety and schizophrenia.  After getting laid off she had an schizophrenic episode.

Please help us locate her...  She has no mobile phone or money so we suspect that he could not be far from the Ballard area unless she was picked up by unknown persons.  

Sorry I made a mistake earlier on the earlier post.  Her DOB is 5-10-97

Please call Seattle PD if you see her at 911 emergency or call me 415-510-1177 Mr Benjamin. 

PLEASE REPOST to all you social networks… specially local SEATTLE networks.

Please… any lead is good. You can call officer Saurbier at 206-684-0850 if you have any leads. 

On a side note, Seattle PD was amazing spending 1 1/2 hours doing an investigation with us. They were amazing.  Officer Saurbier and his team were thorough and at the same time kind and empathetic. Thank you Seattle PD!!!

Violet Benjamin’s picture:

attachments.ocrText: No photo description available.
No photo description available.
No photo description available.

topComments.text:
UPDATE Mar 5 12:18PM Got a message from the Harbor Police through the gentleman who found her clothes.  They got in contact with the boat owner who had CCTV.  Unfortunately they were not able to see anything.  So the theory now is her clothes were thrown over the fence.  Instead of her coming from the INSIDE of the dock harbor, the thought now is someone threw her clothes over the fence.  We really need to the the CCTV of the businesses around the area.   

Our search continues.

UPDATE: Mar 5 6:31 AM. We got a call at 7:45 PM yesterday March 4. It was from a gentleman. We will not identify as this is part of a criminal investigation. The Kind gentleman found violet wet clothing behind the docks by the Ballard boat ramp which is behind the PCC in Ballard. The clothes were found by the gentlemen on Sunday, which was a bright sunny day, and the clothes were sopping wet. They were indeed violet’s clothes. It was everything she wore as if it was stripped off of her, or she stripped it off herself and changed to another set of clothes.

 I can’t tell you what else was found but the police came and picked it up for evidence processing. We think that there’s still a possibility that she’s roaming around in the area. But finding her clothes was devastating to me her father.  

My family and I thank all of you for the love and sport and prayers. Please continue to pray. We will continue to find her, hoping that she just changed her clothes. I guess if there’s a bit of good news, is that there were a lot of cameras in that area and hopefully somebody or some system captured some image.

Some strange things. It seemed that it was all her clothes that she was wearing. And they were sopping wet. They were sopping wet on a sunny day. Also, the clothes were found behind the gates of the boat ramp. Boat ramp gates are one way you cannot get in without a key, but you can get out easily with no key needed. The clothes were found behind the gate. We are hoping that violet put on spare clothes.

It would’ve been strange to have a fully naked woman walking around and not cause a scene. So there is hope that she changed her clothes. We are certain that she had a backpack when she left the house so maybe she had a change of clothes. She also had teddy bear that she kept and held close whenever she was stressed. 

Despite this, we’re still hoping to find her. She is still a missing person. 

If there are eyes that you can spare in a minute or two to help search in the area by Ballard, PCC. And surrounding areas, we would appreciate it.

May your families never experience the pain we’re going through. But our family will be strong. We love Hannah Violet and her family will remain strong for her.

Thank you to all who kept giving us emotional support. Thank you to all who looked at the flyers. We will keep you updated.

This is now in the hands of the seattle police, and we hope that they do their jobs to find violet  

She is still a missing person. 

UPDATE: Mar 4 WEDNESDAY 7:45 AN

Yesterday when we were searching for Violet, there were sighting of her in the genenal vicinity of PCC Ballard.  Some unhoused individuals claimed they had seen her.  We are hopeful she is in the radius close to her former place of work.  If anyone has time to lend a pair of eyes and a few minutes to look in the general vicinity, please do not hesitate to do so.  if you see her do not hesitate to call 911 for assistance.

Thank you... the search continues.

UPDATE: Mar 3 TUESDAY 12:20 PM

A kind neighbor from DOVETAIL CONTRACTORS found Violet's license in their parking lot at 1143 NW 45th St... which is close to PCC where Violet used to work.  They found the license 20 minutes ago at approximately 12:00 noon today Mar 3... Our hope is she is in the vicinity. 

If anyone in the vicinity of the Ballard PCC or 1143 NW 45th and if you see her.  Please call 911 immediately.  She may be having an episode.  She is not homeless or unloved...  We are desperately looking for her.  

We may not know all of you but if you're in the area... another pair of eyes and a minute of your precious time might help us find Violet.

Thank you.

Please repost in all seattle neighborhoods. 

My daugther is missing... Her name is Violet Benjamin, dob 5-10-1997, 5'10 1/2" tall, slim build...  brown hair, brown eyes. 

She is a very vulnerable adult and is missing her meds.  She was last seen in Ballard yesterday day by Swedish Hospital on Tallman. She suffers from severe depression, anxiety and schizophrenia.  After getting laid off she had an schizophrenic episode.

Please help us locate her...  She has no mobile phone or money so we suspect that he could not be far from the Ballard area unless she was picked up by unknown persons.  

Sorry I made a mistake earlier on the earlier post.  Her DOB is 5-10-97

Please call Seattle PD if you see her at 911 emergency or call me 415-510-1177 Mr Benjamin. 

PLEASE REPOST to all you social networks… specially local SEATTLE networks.  Our family is pleading that everyone helps post this on their other social networks… specially Seattle networks 

Does anyone have a connection with local TV news or Seattle newspapers???  Please share the story or dm me w their contact info. Thanks. 

Please… any lead is good. You can call officer Saurbier at 206-684-0850 if you have any leads. 

On a side note, Seattle PD was amazing spending 1 1/2 hours doing an investigation with us. They were amazing.  Officer Saurbier and his team were thorough and at the same time kind and empathetic. Thank you Seattle PD!!!

I am praying for Violet🙏 My daughter went to school with her at Seattle U, and was very fond of her. My daughter lives in Tacoma and is aware and looking, along with many of her friends. I cannot imagine the unbearable pain and stress you are going through. I will continue to pray and hope for a great outcome for Violet. Stay strong and have faith🙏

text:
My daughter ran away on 2/22/26 from Tacoma. Her name is Samantha Ellison. She is 17 years old. Height: 5'1", 110lbs, she has a septum piercing and glasses. She's a good kid. She doesn't have any resources and struggles with her mental health. She abandoned her car and shut off her phone.

A police report has been filed, and we've checked with friends and family. No one has seen or heard from her. She has ties to the Seattle area. If you've seen her, please call the Tacoma Police. Case# 26053008055 OR call 911.

Link to share: [[https://www.facebook.com/share/p/15aCtvZHHT7/](https://www.facebook.com/tiff.mcpeak/posts/pfbid02fKbrtLJPrNJ6t5V2MKvKtsJLga5zbxSKAari9CrKTPpC9KRvYLpWky9U9ES2HDQ1l?__cft__%5b0%5d=AZYq2RcZuxmXHLU_1MDHtp2prIGN9HeIZN9dqgRWzUn-y-P8EW_mQLccxOIbACpxT-RCLAvvxJ3EeTv19KrcdASHRRnaexp9qXHIDEkGaCWSQoyNheOjSMR-tWyj_4J10JqQV67b2WAfdYUXBLMHRp7J-9bOKExF1Vjb6VUBBrLenCt9R3RZo9uXqmIxrKffwic&__tn__=-UK-R)]

text:
Hi everyone!

My name is Maria, I’m 28 years old and a former professional basketball player from Russia. I played at a high competitive level in Russia and have extensive game experience.

I’m 5’10” and play as a forward. I read the game well, enjoy team basketball, ball movement, and a fast pace.

I moved to Seattle about six months ago and I’m currently looking for a recreational or semi-competitive team to play with regularly. I’m open to practices and competitive games — just excited to be back on the court 🙂

If your team is looking for a player or you know of a league, I’d love to connect!

topComments.text: Are you looking to connect with people as well, or just play ball?
I know of a coed group that meets on Sundays in west Seattle

topComments.text: what a way to ruin a good ferry ride.

The ferry system is also very helpful if you want to “spread” your loved one’s ashes from the ferry. You can’t just scatter the cremains, they have to be in a non-toxic container that will sink then disintegrate over time on the bottom. It has to be arranged in advance, you specify the route, date and which run, and the approximate point on the route. They stop the ferry after announcing what is happening and asking for consideration for the mourning family. The captain gives a lovely certificate with the date, time and location of the stop. We did this with both my brother then mother off of Ft Flagler on the Port Townsend run.

text:
Hello, guys! I'm living in Shoreline and I'm looking at the possibility of starting my career in civil construction here in the USA. I would like to ask some questions to those who are already in the area:
How is the market for those who are starting now as an absolute beginner, without previous experience or certifications? Is it very difficult to get the first opportunity as a laborer?
In addition, my long-term goal is to become a Construction Manager if I proceed on this area  . I know that many go up in their careers for practical experience, without necessarily having a university degree. Did anyone here follow this path? I would love more information about it

topComments.text: What do you mean by civil construction? Like mass infrastructure and industrial? What kind of work are you trying to do?

Have you thought about going to somewhere where there is more growth?  I live in the greater Tucson area and construction here is very busy.  There is a lot of growth. It’s a smaller city. Much lower cost of living than Seattle. After living in Seattle for 13 years, we came back to Tucson. Slower pace. Lower cost of living and the growth here is happening. Good for construction folks.

Heading to Seattle this summer for my internship and I’m hyped to experience the culture out there!

topComments.text:
Pikes place market has a really yummy turkey leg/bbq spot. Don’t remember the name but it’s right up front near the bronze pig. There’s also a good little ethnic store. They have some Colombian food, Mexican food. My son got some fresh empanadas there. You won’t find really great Mexican food (I lived there for a year and nothing compared to Colorado) but they have other amazing things to try. I def want to move back! 
Go to pikes place and support the locals for sure.
What part of town are we talking about?  Seattle is a big place.

text:
Update: Thank you to everyone who took time to comment & give suggestions. As a Floridian I am super excited to come enjoy your weather for a week, along with the beautiful scenery and enjoy local food! Can’t wait to see the Pacific Ocean 😊

My husband & I are traveling to Seattle from Florida next week. We rented a car and are wanting to find the best place to go to enjoy the Pacific Ocean that isn’t too far of a drive (no more than 2.5-3 hours). I was sold on Westport Light State Park until I found Ocean Shores. Now I’ve seen not so good reviews for that. Any recommendations would be helpful and appreciated.

topComments.text: Cannon Beach, OR is 3 1/2 hours. Worth the drive - amazingly beautiful beach and town. Spend one night at The Waves.  You won’t regret it!

attachments.ocrText: No photo description available.

topComments.text: Go enjoy Alki beach in West Seattle. Have lunch at Salty’s on Alki and enjoy the best view of the city. Take the ferry to Bainbridge Island. I have lived here all of my life. Also rode the ferries all of my life as my dad grew up on the Island and all my family was there. The views leaving the city and coming back? Spectacular. Every time I have gone to Ocean Shores, the weather has been cold, windy and crappy, Went to Westport and camped as a kid. Never had a desire to go back. A Day trip to Leavenworth is always fun. Our own ‘Little Bavaria’. I have been to Bavaria. This is great. Perhaps experience Whale watching? Puget Sound Express in Edmonds is awesome.

text: Will be visiting Feb 20-23rd what’s the weather like and what do you recommend to do?

topComments.text: Go to the Seattle Waterfront. Aquarium. Ye ' Ol Curiosity Shop. Visit the shops. Where are you staying?

You just can’t tell. But if you’ve come from a cold weather area, it will seem mild. Aurora Ave and White Center was a joke, although we frequently go to White Center. It’s always been a landing spot for new immigrants, so it’s very diverse. Walking the miles-long waterfront hiking trail around the West Seattle peninsula is extremely scenic and popular with the locals. The east side of the walk looks across Elliott Bay at the Space Needle and Seattle skyline. Continuing around Duwamish Head, the view changes to looking west across Puget Sound at the Olympic Mountain peaks.

sharedPost.text:
Feb. 11th at 6:30 a.m., my boys left the house to go watch the Super Bowl parade. By 8 a.m., they had secured a front-line spot, ready for the experience of a lifetime.

He brought his NFL football with him… and then his wildest dreams came true. As the parade was passing, Antonio threw the ball to Charbonnet. Him, Woolen and another special teams player signed it and tossed it back. My son was absolutely elated. It was a moment he will never forget.

Later, when Sam Darnold passed by, they attempted to toss the ball again — but at the same time, someone else threw a football and the two collided, my son’s ball was lost in the crowd. 

What started as one of the most amazing days of his life ended in tears. He was crushed. 💔

I tried consoling him reminding that he will be at the Smith-Njigba Football Camp this summer, still chasing those dreams — but he can’t help missing that special ball and the memory attached to it. 🏈 

If anyone happened to pick up a signed NFL football in the crowd that day, it would mean the world to a young fan to have it returned. 🙏

#Seahawks #SeahawksNation #seahawksfan #seattleseahawks #Charbonnet #SamDarnold
#SuperBowl #SeattleWashington #seattle #Super Bowl

topComments.text: Lets get this young lad his ball back if not lets get him another 1 sighned by all x
If it is signed, you might not get it back since it’s worth some money. Might have better luck sending a message and photos to Seahawks headquarters. Goodluck

text:
I know it's a long shot but my son’s wallet was lost on Friday, January 30th, and we’re really hoping a kind, honest person may have picked it up.

🖤 Description:
• Black bifold wallet
• May contain his ID and debit card and other various cards 

📍 Possible locations:
Anywhere from Seattle to Federal Way, but he believes he most likely lost it in Seattle while he was working door to door sales. Last known location was around Noon in Seattle. 

If you happened to find it, or even saw one matching this description, it would mean the world to us to get it back. Even an upvote or share helps this reach the right person. 💙

Thank you so much, neighbors—this community has come through before, and we truly appreciate any help!

topComments.text: Adding area in Seattle where he was working might help.

text:
Hi everyone! 🌎🚐
I’m planning a road trip in my 24-ft long and 11-ft high RV across the United States this summer, and I’m planning to be in Seattle on June 24. I want to spend a few hours exploring the city that day.

Does anyone have suggestions on where I can park my RV for a few hours (ideally safe, reasonably priced, and within easy reach of downtown/Space Needle/Pike Place Market)? Any tips on good day-parking spots, RV-friendly lots, or apps/sites you use for this would be hugely appreciated!

Thanks so much! 😊✌️

topComments.text:
Park in Seattle on the street somewhere near the baseball park - 1st Ave just north the street is wide enough and just pay per hour electronically in an app.  Only issue is you are limited to 2 hours there unless a Sunday or holiday when no charge or limit.

Buses run along First and can easily get to Pike Place.  Space Needle is further away yet you can still get there by bus.  Around the Needle, also street parking, just might be harder to find a space since there is a lot of housing there and the roads are not as easy to navigate - lot of trees on the side where branches may get in your way.  (I know because we have traveled all over the U.S. in a 24’ RV and have driven into a number of cities.)

Open surface lots that could take an RV would be nearer the ballpark, but not really anything near Pike Place.  Some surface lots around Lake Union and other areas around the outskirts but many are small and tight.  

One other idea you could park on Mercer Island (7-8 mi from downtown) on the street but time limited or possibly in the open surface lot (free too) near the new light rail station.  The light rail is set to be running into Seattle over Lake Washington the end of March.  The surface lot is mostly designed for cars but you might be able to use two adjacent front to back spaces.  Worth checking into.
Airport..take tram downtown
